{{Quote|Please don't hurt me— I just serve the food here, that's all! I'm not like these others— I'm not even a Black Vulkar.|Ada to Revan|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
'''Ada''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who was [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] under the [[Black Vulkars/Legends|Black Vulkars]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] to settle her [[Ada's father|father's]] [[Currency/Legends|debt]]. In [[3956 BBY]], during the [[Jedi Civil War]], she encountered [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], who freed her from enslavement.

==Biography==
{{Quote|My father owed Davik some money, but he couldn't pay. Davik killed my father and sold me into slavery here at the Vulkar compound to pay off the debt.|Ada speaking to Revan about her father|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
Ada was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who lived during the [[Jedi Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. Her [[Ada's father|father]] owed [[Currency/Legends|money]] to [[Davik Kang]], the local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime-lord]] for the [[Exchange]], a powerful and [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] spanning criminal organization. To settle the debt, Kang had Ada's [[Parent/Legends|father]] [[Death/Legends|killed]] and sold Ada into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] to the [[Black Vulkars/Legends|Black Vulkars]], a [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]] that operated in the [[Lower City]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|city world]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. She was forced to work as a [[Waiter/Legends|waitress]] in the [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang's]] [[Black Vulkar Base|compound]], where she was regularly harassed by the Vulkars.<ref name="KOTOR" />
{{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
In [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', in which Ada appears, in [[3956 BBY]].</ref> the former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], who [[Amnesia/Legends|lost]] his memories and believed himself to be a [[Republic soldier]], entered the Vulkar base searching for a [[Prototype accelerator|prototype]] [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] bike accelerator that had been stolen from the [[Hidden Beks]], a [[Bek–Vulkar gang war|rival]] [[Swoop racing/Legends|swoop]] gang. Revan killed many of the guards in the base and eventually encountered Ada. She begged Revan not to kill her, but he assured her he wouldn't and instead wanted to ask questions. Ada told Revan about her father's debt and her ensuing enslavement. Revan asked her about the prototype engine and the location of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Bastila Shan]], captured and assumed by the Vulkars to be a [[Republic officer/Legends|Republic officer]]. Ada was unable to offer much information, but overheard some of the gang members talk about Shan being held off-base. After giving as much information as she could, Ada was allowed to leave. With the Vulkars guarding her now dead, she was able to find her way out of the base and fled into the Lower City, now a free woman.<ref name="KOTOR" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
Ada appeared as a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', developed by [[BioWare]]. Interactions with her are optional and do not affect quests. If the player completes the "Search for Bastila" quest, Ada will disappear and can not be interacted with. When the player speaks to her, they may choose to free her and receive light side points or kill her and shift towards the dark side. If Carth Onasi is in the player's party when they attempt to kill her, he will prevent them from doing so and allow Ada to flee.<ref name="KOTOR" />
